FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class AnimEngine

Namespace
FluentGpu.Animation
Assembly
FluentGpu.Animation.dll

Generic, composition-style animation runtime (phase 7) — the Web-Animations model on a fixed channel set. Tracks are eased (multi-keyframe + per-segment easing, time- OR driven-timeline) or springs (ODE with velocity handoff on retarget). Per (node,channel) tracks combine by Replace/Add/Accumulate. Each tick composes the surviving channel values into NodePaint.LocalTransform (T∘R∘S) + Opacity and marks Transform/PaintDirty — animation NEVER relays out.

public sealed class AnimEngine

Properties

Clocks 

public DrivenClockTable Clocks { get; }

Property Value

DrivenClockTable

HasActive 

public bool HasActive { get; }

Property Value

bool

IncrementalRoots

Nodes whose presented SIZE changed this tick under SizeMode.Relayout — the host re-solves just these subtrees (scoped layout) so their text re-wraps live. Cleared by the host after it consumes them.

public List<NodeHandle> IncrementalRoots { get; }

Property Value

List<NodeHandle>

LoopTrackCount

Looping tracks (Loop==true) — O(1) maintained counter, not a scan.

public int LoopTrackCount { get; }

Property Value

int

ReflowRoots

Nodes whose REFLOW size advanced this tick — the host re-solves their boundary scope, then refreshes the Trailing child-shift from the fresh bounds. Cleared by the host after it consumes them.

public List<NodeHandle> ReflowRoots { get; }

Property Value

List<NodeHandle>

TrackCount

Active animation tracks (springs + eased/driven), all channels — O(1) census.

public int TrackCount { get; }

Property Value

int

TransitionCount

Live per-node layout-transition specs (the _transitions side-table) — O(1) census.

public int TransitionCount { get; }

Property Value

int

Methods

Animate(NodeHandle, AnimChannel, float, float, float, Easing, CompositeOp, float)

Eased two-point tween (retargets any live track on the same node+channel).

public void Animate(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel, float from, float to, float durationMs, Easing easing = Easing.EaseInOut, CompositeOp composite = CompositeOp.Replace, float delayMs = 0)

node NodeHandle
channel AnimChannel
from float
to float
durationMs float
easing Easing
composite CompositeOp
delayMs float

public void Animate(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel, float from, float to, float durationMs, EasingSpec easing, CompositeOp composite = CompositeOp.Replace, float delayMs = 0)

Parameters

node NodeHandle
channel AnimChannel
from float
to float
durationMs float
easing EasingSpec
composite CompositeOp
delayMs float

AnimateBounds(NodeHandle, in RectF, in RectF, in LayoutTransition)

FLIP the node from its captured presented rect to its new laid-out rect, seeding/retargeting the channels the spec requests. Position is velocity-continuous: a running spring keeps its velocity and shifts its offset by the layout delta (no jump on interruption); a fresh spring starts offset by the full delta and settles to 0. The host calls this once per commit for every BoundsAnimated node that moved; Tick then advances it every frame.

public void AnimateBounds(NodeHandle node, in RectF fromAbs, in RectF toAbs, in LayoutTransition spec)

Parameters

node NodeHandle
fromAbs RectF
toAbs RectF
spec LayoutTransition

Cancel(NodeHandle, AnimChannel) 

public void Cancel(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel)

Parameters

node NodeHandle
channel AnimChannel

CancelAll(NodeHandle) 

public void CancelAll(NodeHandle node)

Parameters

node NodeHandle

CancelToRest(NodeHandle, AnimChannel)

Cancel + reset the channel's paint to its settle-time resting sentinel — symmetric with the settle path (StrokeTrim/PresentedW/H → NaN, Clip → Infinite). A bare Cancel(NodeHandle, AnimChannel) only drops the track, freezing the last interpolated value in paint with no spec fallback (a deactivated ProgressRing froze a partial arc). Channels without a rest sentinel (transform/opacity/layout) behave exactly like Cancel — their resting value is the reconciler's static re-assert.

public void CancelToRest(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel)

Parameters

node NodeHandle
channel AnimChannel

ClearTransition(NodeHandle)

Drop a node's layout-transition spec (the element stopped declaring Animate, or the slot was freed).

public void ClearTransition(NodeHandle node)

Parameters

node NodeHandle

ConsumeReflowWrites()

True if any reflow track wrote LayoutInput this tick (interp advance or settle restore) — the host runs a boundary-scoped re-solve before record. Self-clearing.

public bool ConsumeReflowWrites()

Returns

bool

Drive(NodeHandle, AnimChannel, Keyframe[], int, float, float, CompositeOp)

Scroll/value-driven track: progress comes from a DrivenClock source mapped through [domainMin,domainMax].

public void Drive(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel, Keyframe[] keys, int drivenRef, float domainMin, float domainMax, CompositeOp composite = CompositeOp.Replace)

Parameters

node NodeHandle
channel AnimChannel
keys Keyframe[]
drivenRef int
domainMin float
domainMax float
composite CompositeOp

Evaluate an easing curve (kept for source compatibility; the implementation lives in Foundation).

public static float Ease(Easing e, float t)

Parameters

e Easing
t float

Returns

float

HasTracks(NodeHandle)

True while any track targets this node (used by the host to detect a settled exit orphan).

public bool HasTracks(NodeHandle node)

Parameters

node NodeHandle

Returns

bool

Keyframes(NodeHandle, AnimChannel, Keyframe[], float, bool, CompositeOp, float)

Multi-keyframe eased track (@keyframes). Offsets must be ascending in 0..1.

public void Keyframes(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel, Keyframe[] keys, float durationMs, bool loop = false, CompositeOp composite = CompositeOp.Replace, float delayMs = 0)

Parameters

node NodeHandle
channel AnimChannel
keys Keyframe[]
durationMs float
loop bool
composite CompositeOp
delayMs float

SeedEnter(NodeHandle, in EnterExit, in LayoutTransition)

An inserted node animates FROM the enter terminal (offset/scale/opacity) TO identity.

public void SeedEnter(NodeHandle node, in EnterExit e, in LayoutTransition spec)

Parameters

node NodeHandle
e EnterExit
spec LayoutTransition

SeedExit(NodeHandle, in EnterExit, in LayoutTransition)

A removed node (now an Exiting orphan) animates FROM its current state TO the exit terminal; when all its tracks settle (HasTracks(NodeHandle) == false) the host reclaims it.

public void SeedExit(NodeHandle node, in EnterExit e, in LayoutTransition spec)

Parameters

node NodeHandle
e EnterExit
spec LayoutTransition

SetTransition(NodeHandle, in LayoutTransition)

Attach (or replace) a node's layout-transition spec. Called by the reconciler from BoxEl.Animate.

public void SetTransition(NodeHandle node, in LayoutTransition t)

Parameters

node NodeHandle
t LayoutTransition

Spring(NodeHandle, AnimChannel, float, in SpringParams, float?, CompositeOp, float)

Spring toward to. If a spring already runs on this node+channel, it RETARGETS — keeping position + velocity (no snap), the iOS/Compose velocity-handoff.

public void Spring(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel, float to, in SpringParams spring, float? initial = null, CompositeOp composite = CompositeOp.Replace, float delayMs = 0)

Parameters

node NodeHandle
channel AnimChannel
to float
spring SpringParams
initial float?
composite CompositeOp
delayMs float

Tick(float) 

public void Tick(float dtMs)

Parameters

dtMs float

TryGetTrackValue(NodeHandle, AnimChannel, out float)

The LIVE value of an in-flight track on node+channel — so an interrupting eased tween can start FROM where the animation currently is instead of a recomputed endpoint (WinUI AnimatedIcon queues/blends transitions, AnimatedIcon.cpp:235-267 — a mid-flight chevron reversal must not snap). False = no live track (caller uses its resting value).

public bool TryGetTrackValue(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel, out float value)

Parameters

node NodeHandle
channel AnimChannel
value float

Returns

bool

TryGetTransition(NodeHandle, out LayoutTransition)

Read a node's layout-transition spec (set by the reconciler this commit).

public bool TryGetTransition(NodeHandle node, out LayoutTransition t)

Parameters

node NodeHandle
t LayoutTransition

Returns

bool
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
